mapping.a_element: large N scaling function in a double scaling limit for Wilson loop operators
mapping.b_element: double scaling limit in large-N properties of unitary-matrix models

mapping.a_element: eigenvalue distribution of Wilson loop operator
mapping.b_element: eigenvalue distribution in single-link integral (unitary-matrix models)

mapping.a_element: universality class of phase transitions in large N QCD
mapping.b_element: universality in large-N expansion of unitary-matrix models (e.g., YM_2, chiral models)

mapping.a_element: transition under scaling at infinite N
mapping.b_element: phase transitions or critical behavior in the large-N limit of matrix models

brief_justification: Both papers investigate phase transitions or critical behavior in large-N limits of matrix models, with a focus on double scaling limits and universality classes. Paper A's Wilson loop operator eigenvalue distribution and its scaling function correspond to Paper B's analysis of eigenvalue distributions and double scaling in unitary-matrix models, particularly in the context of YM_2 and chiral models.
